description_long: This object is a pair of pliers with a sleek, angular design, primarily colored in black with blue accents at the handle tips and pivot area. The handles are long and straight, slightly tapering towards the ends, and appear to be ergonomically shaped for a comfortable grip. The jaws are narrow and pointed, suggesting a needle-nose style, ideal for precision work such as gripping small objects or bending wire. The pivot mechanism is centrally located, allowing the two arms to move smoothly and providing a strong clamping force at the jaws. The surface finish is smooth and hard, likely made from metal with some plastic or rubberized elements for the handle grips. No visible branding or logos are present on the exterior surfaces.
description: A sleek, black and blue pair of needle-nose pliers with long, straight handles and pointed jaws, featuring a central pivot and ergonomic grip.
description_view_0: Top-down view showing the V-shaped handles and the pointed, narrow jaws clearly.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thin profile of the pliers and the alignment of the handles and jaws.
description_view_2: Another top-down view, nearly identical to view 0, emphasizing the symmetry and handle design.
description_view_3: Side view similar to view 1, showing the slimness and straight alignment of the handles.
attribute_handle_type: Long, straight, slightly tapered handles with blue plastic or rubberized ergonomic grips.
attribute_jaw_design: Narrow, pointed needle-nose jaws with a fine tip for precision gripping; no visible serrations or wire cutter.
attribute_pivot_mechanism: Fixed central pivot allowing standard opening and closing motion; no visible slip-joint or adjustable mechanism.
attribute_grip_pattern: Smooth with possible slight texturing from the blue grip material; no pronounced ribbing or knurling.
